name: Overall LEV Outlook
summary: Current public outlook for overall progress toward longevity escape velocity.
main_blockers: The field still lacks replicated human functional gains that clearly stack toward long-run LEV.

Recent coverage-repair publications improve source completeness and branch boundaries, but many track baselines remain low-confidence, biomarker-heavy, animal-stage, trial-watch-only, or disease-specific rather than direct aging efficacy.
best_current_signals: All 38 seeded tracks now have public baseline outlooks, and early coverage repairs are tightening support maps without requiring a forecast upgrade.
Multiple hallmarks now include human biomarker, functional, or disease-outcome signals, even though most remain low-confidence.
forecast_note: The June baseline and coverage-repair passes make the public map more complete and better bounded, not more optimistic. The center of gravity remains early human translation: enough human biomarker, functional, disease-specific outcome, and trial-watch evidence to track seriously, but not enough replicated aging-directed functional benefit to imply escape velocity.
